38299:
[Lr. from Secretary of Congress Oct 21<sup>st</sup> 1786.]

(Circular)

Office of Secretary of Congress
October 21.<sup>st</sup> 1786 —

Sir

I have the honor of transmitting to Your Excellency herewith enclosed, an Act of the United States in Congress Assembled for augmenting the Troops in the service of the Union to the number of 2040 Non commissioned Officers & privates which was passed by the unanimous vote of the ten States now represented in Congress —

For Your further information I herewith enclose the Intelligence on which the report of the Committee is grounded —

with the greatest respect
I have the honor, to be
Your Excellency's
Most Obedient &
Most humble Servant

Cha Thomson

His Excellency
The Governor
of
Virginia —
39604:
[W<sup>m</sup> Knox — War Office
Oct. 2<sup>d</sup> 1788]

[War Office
2]

The Hon<sup>ble</sup>
Jeremiah Wadsworth Esquire
Hartford
Connecticut

N York Oct 28

War Office 28<sup>th</sup> Oct<sup>r</sup> 1788

Sir/

I have to aknowledge the receipt this day of One Hundred and Fifty pounds Lawful Money all in Gold, and of a draft on Richard Platt Esq<sup>r</sup> for One thousand Dollars brought from Hartford by Cap<sup>t</sup> W<sup>m</sup> Price — M<sup>r</sup> Platt had previously informed me that he had a thousand Dollars Subject to my order in consequence of a letter he received from you on the 25<sup>th</sup> Instant.

In the absence of the Secretary at War
I have the Honor to be
Sir, Your Most Obed<sup>t</sup> Servant

W<sup>m</sup> Knox

[different handwriting:] NB. The above sums of £450 —
were charged Gen<sup>l</sup> Knox the 23 Oct<sup>r</sup> 1788

Col<sup>o</sup> J. Wadsworth.

PS. As Gen<sup>l</sup> Knox is to remain at Boston some time longer I beg you to send on under another cover the letter directed for him to your care at Hartford.
38728:
[Copy Letter to Gen<sup>l</sup> Knox
21<sup>st</sup> May 1787 —]

Providence 21<sup>st</sup> May 1787

Sir

your fav<sup>r</sup> of 9<sup>th</sup> Ins<sup>t</sup> has been Rec<sup>d</sup> I shall begin tomorrow the Examination of the public Stores — the principle part of wich are in this Town. Some at Warren — Bristol Greenwich & at Fort Island in Newport Harbor, as the Complating this business will Necessarily be attended with some expence for Labourers Horse hire &c. and it being out of my power to make the Necessary advances — I must Request you will be so good as to forward Fifteen Dollars for the first Conveyance for which I will Stand accountable — I shall go on with the Examination of the Stores in this Town, if I can Engage two or three hands, before the money arrives —

I am S<sup>r</sup>
with Real Esteem
your most Obed<sup>t</sup> Hum<sup>l</sup> Ser<sup>t</sup>
Jer<sup>eh</sup> Olney —

Gen<sup>l</sup> Knox
Secretary at War —
39514:
[page number:] 25

(Duplicate)

War Office September 23<sup>d</sup> 1788

Sir

In the absence of the Secretary at War I had the honor to write you on the 22<sup>d</sup> Ultimo, at the same time enclosed you a resolve of Congress of the 20<sup>th</sup> Ultimo, appointing you Agent for the Chickasaw nation of Indians the first was addressed to the care of, and to be forwarded by the post master at Richmond, and a duplicate to Hillsborough North Carolina with similar directions.

I now transmit you a resolve of Congress of the 5<sup>th</sup> Instant accompanying a Proclamation of the same date which is intended to have an extensive circulation, and therefore I take the liberty to enclose you ten copies.

I have the Honor to be
Sir, Your Most Ob<sup>t</sup> Servant
W<sup>m</sup> Knox

Colonel Joseph Martin
Agent for the Chickasaw & Cherokee
Nations of Indians

Sir

I do myself the Honor to Inclose you a List of Warrants drawn at this Office on account of the Pay of the Foreign Officers amounting to 25,516.<sup>79</sup> Dollars.
37026:
[M<sup>r</sup> Morris —
Oct<sup>r</sup> 1<sup>st</sup> 1784
Pierce]

Pay Office Philad<sup>a</sup> October 1.<sup>st</sup> 1784

Sir

The Secretary in the War Office has drawn a Warrant on me for Four hundred & fifty five dollars, being the Subsistence of the Officers of the Pennsylvania Troops for the Months of October, November & December 1784

I am to request the above Sum may be granted       I have the Honor to be

Sir
your most Obedient
Jn<sup>o</sup> Pierce

The Honorable
Superintendent of Finance
